festivals.name: Carnaval de Oruro
festivals.description: UNESCO's masterpiece of oral heritage: 10,000 dancers in elaborate costumes parade for 20 hours in a mining city.

festivals.name: Alasitas
festivals.description: La Paz's festival of abundance where Ekeko figurines and miniature items are bought to manifest desires into reality.

destinations.name: Salar de Uyuni
destinations.description: The world's largest salt flat creates perfect mirror reflections at dawn, with flamingo-filled lagoons at the edge.

destinations.name: La Paz
destinations.description: The world's highest capital at 3,650m has a spectacular cable car network, Witch's Market, and lunar valley nearby.

destinations.name: Death Road (Yungas)
destinations.description: The world's most dangerous road drops 3,500m from cold Andean peaks into warm jungle — a legendary mountain bike ride.

destinations.name: Lake Titicaca (Copacabana)
destinations.description: Bolivia's shore of the sacred lake leads to Isla del Sol, sacred birthplace of the Inca sun god.

destinations.name: Sucre
destinations.description: The constitutional capital — a UNESCO World Heritage white colonial city where Bolivia's independence was declared.

destinations.name: Potosí
destinations.description: A UNESCO city built on silver at 4,090m — the world's highest city — with an infamous working mine tour.

destinations.name: Tiwanaku
destinations.description: The spiritual and political centre of a pre-Inca civilization that dominated the Andes for 400 years.

destinations.name: Oruro
destinations.description: A high-altitude mining city that transforms into a carnival spectacle each February with UNESCO-recognized celebrations.
